{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Frankfort horizontal line is a well-established anatomical reference extending from the superior border of the external auditory canal to the inferior orbital rim. However, Document [24] (Title: Facial Chin Augmentation -- Preparation) states 'Furthermore, radiographic evaluation, including panoramic radiographs and/or lateral and anterior-posterior (AP) radiographs, may be beneficial for addressing more complex deformities such as vertical height excess, orthognathic abnormalities, and transverse asymmetry, commonly observed in conditions such as hemifacial/craniofacial microsomia, oculoauricular vertebral (OAV) syndrome, or isolated anatomical components.' This clearly indicates that the Frankfort horizontal line is constructed by joining the nasion and the inferior orbital rim. Therefore, the correct answer is C: Porion and Nasion.", "answer_choice": "C"}
